title: Yolo

jokes.text: D'oh! Not again!
jokes.setup: Opening scene with Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.punchline: Homer's iconic 'D'oh!' catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration with Homer's iconic catchphrase, economical delivery

jokes.text: Why are we best friends? Because your seat was behind mine!
jokes.setup: Bart and Milhouse fighting over who goes first on a ride
jokes.punchline: Absurd explanation that their friendship is based on seating arrangement
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic about the arbitrary nature of childhood friendships

jokes.text: Dear Weirdo, pick up weirdo kid. And send.
jokes.setup: Marge dealing with an endless playdate
jokes.punchline: Brutally direct text message to Kirk
jokes.explanation: Marge's uncharacteristic bluntness is funny, economical delivery

jokes.text: Did you eat a peanut? No, I just sniffed a nectarine. It's okay. I can breathe through my tear ducts.
jokes.setup: Milhouse having an allergic reaction
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd details about his severe allergies and adaptation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character-specific humor about Milhouse's health issues

jokes.text: Tell me if this gets annoying. I think I'm gonna throw up the mac and cheese you fed me. I'm not supposed to have it. That's why I had so much.
jokes.setup: Milhouse politely asking if he's being annoying while being maximally annoying
jokes.punchline: The backwards logic of eating more of what he's not supposed to have
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Milhouse's oblivious nature and kid logic

jokes.text: If this kid's dad isn't here in one second, he's going in the garbage can.
jokes.setup: Marge's patience worn thin with Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Threatening to literally throw a child away
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation of parental frustration

jokes.text: Hey, guys! Who wants a whiff of New Kirk Smell?
jokes.setup: Kirk arriving to pick up Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Embarrassingly trying to show off his 'new' self
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kirk Van Houten cringe behavior, very character-specific

jokes.text: Once again, cats have it better.
jokes.setup: Learning about 'YOLO' - You Only Live Once
jokes.punchline: Homer's observation about cats having nine lives
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective observational humor with good economy

jokes.text: Please. Just 'cause I bought a new car, lost a little weight and started taking a DJ class, everyone thinks I'm having a midlife crisis.
jokes.setup: Kirk denying he's having a midlife crisis
jokes.punchline: While listing textbook midlife crisis behaviors
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic denial while proving the opposite, good structure

jokes.text: Dad! You got me a skateboard? It's for me. Now get in the back. And while you're there, use this cream to massage the leather.
jokes.setup: Milhouse excited about getting a skateboard
jokes.punchline: Kirk's selfish revelation and treating his son like a servant
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Kirk's narcissistic midlife crisis behavior

jokes.text: Look at me! I'm afraid of dying.
jokes.setup: Homer watching Kirk's midlife crisis display
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Homer's direct, honest self-awareness is funny and economical

jokes.text: You've had the same job, same car, same house for 20 years. And that's all you'll ever have. A cycle you'll never ever, ever, ever, ever, ever change. And you're okay with it!
jokes.setup: Marge trying to comfort Homer about his contentment
jokes.punchline: The mounting repetition of 'ever' making it more depressing than comforting
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure that turns comfort into existential dread

jokes.text: Like I say night after night after night... nighty-night.
jokes.setup: Homer's response to Marge's depressing comfort
jokes.punchline: Even his bedtime routine emphasizes the endless repetition
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure extending the repetition joke

jokes.text: I thought this was a puff piece. You're wearing a sweater.
jokes.setup: Skinner expecting a friendly news story
jokes.punchline: Using Kent's casual attire as evidence of the story's tone
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about dress codes signaling story tone

jokes.text: This journalism just turned... gotcha!
jokes.setup: Kent revealing this is an investigative story
jokes.punchline: Dramatic pause before the anticlimatic 'gotcha'
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of sensational journalism tropes

jokes.text: People, make room for your local Emmy nomination certificates. No, do not make room.
jokes.setup: Kent's confidence about winning awards
jokes.punchline: Immediate self-correction acknowledging local news limitations
jokes.explanation: Good self-deflating humor about local news delusions of grandeur

jokes.text: Myra, don't I have another appointment? Oh, this is your first appointment ever.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to escape the interview
jokes.punchline: Secretary revealing he never has appointments
jokes.explanation: Good character moment exposing Skinner's pathetic professional life

jokes.text: This school is more corrupt than the Italian parliament.
jokes.setup: Kent commenting on the school's corruption
jokes.punchline: Hyperbolic comparison to notoriously corrupt institution
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation joke, relies on cultural reference

jokes.text: You can make a lot of cabbage betting on K-ball.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to bribe Kent with inside information
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of gambling on elementary school kickball
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about the low stakes of elementary school

jokes.text: Yeah, well, at least you got your health, huh? Now let's see if I can take that away from you. Your poison.
jokes.setup: Moe's attempt at consoling Homer
jokes.punchline: Immediately threatening to poison him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character comedy - dark humor with good structure

jokes.text: I can't believe I'm saying this, but I'm starting to regret saying 'YOLO.'
jokes.setup: Kirk's midlife crisis antics
jokes.punchline: Regretting the philosophy that started his transformation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to episode's theme with character awareness

jokes.text: Back in fifth grade, you either had to write to a foreigner or a prisoner. I picked a foreigner because the prisoners wrote back too fast.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his pen pal choice
jokes.punchline: Rejecting prisoners for being too responsive, not too dangerous
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - subverts expectation about why prisoners would be avoided

jokes.text: I was gonna be King of Cheeseburger Mountain.
jokes.setup: Homer discussing childhood dreams with Eduardo
jokes.punchline: His absurd but perfectly Homer-like aspiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - absurd but totally believable for Homer

jokes.text: Did I change or did they stop making mountains out of cheeseburgers? Probably a little bit of both.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on why his dream didn't come true
jokes.punchline: Sincere consideration that cheeseburger mountains might have existed
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic showing Homer's sincere belief in impossible things

jokes.text: You know, maybe this so-called cheating scandal is actually an opportunity to, uh... ...initiate a-a dialogue that would, uh, create a teachable moment. Uh, something, something buzz word... I got nothing.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to use educational jargon to deflect
jokes.punchline: Complete breakdown and admission of having nothing
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing bureaucratic BS falling apart under pressure

jokes.text: That's it? That's all you've got? Something I can read on a tea box?
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting an honor code as solution
jokes.punchline: Dismissing it as generic wisdom found on tea packaging
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about generic inspirational sayings

jokes.text: I did it. I saved the day. The same way I won that battle in Vietnam. By fainting!
jokes.setup: Skinner taking credit for Lisa's solution
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to his military 'heroics' through fainting
jokes.explanation: Great character callback with dark humor about his war experience

jokes.text: If you're my wife's secret lover, come in. There's nothing I can do for her anymore.
jokes.setup: Eduardo arriving at Kirk's door
jokes.punchline: Kirk's defeated assumption about why a man would visit
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Kirk's complete defeat and self-loathing

jokes.text: Ay, dios mio! What has happened to you? Did your hair burn off in a fire that trapped you in a candy factory?
jokes.setup: Eduardo seeing adult Homer for the first time
jokes.punchline: Wildly specific theory about Homer's baldness and weight
jokes.explanation: Creative and specific visual description that's absurdly elaborate

jokes.text: It's so nice to meet a friend Homer met through the mail who isn't a sea monkey.
jokes.setup: Marge meeting Eduardo
jokes.punchline: Implying Homer has ordered sea monkeys as pen pals before
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor suggesting Homer's history of poor pen pal choices

jokes.text: Eduardo Barcelona. Or in English, Eddie Miami.
jokes.setup: Eduardo introducing himself
jokes.punchline: Translating city names rather than the actual name
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay showing misunderstanding of how name translation works

jokes.text: Ooh! I've had eight wives and 200 children! Among them artists, doctors and revolutionary chefs.
jokes.setup: Homer mentioning his family as his treasure
jokes.punchline: Eduardo's dramatically excessive life accomplishments
jokes.explanation: Good escalation contrasting Eduardo's wild success with Homer's modest life

jokes.text: Do you have a disrespectful son who calls you by your first name? I cannot imagine such a creature.
jokes.setup: Eduardo asking about disrespectful children
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to fathom such behavior
jokes.explanation: Sets up Bart's immediate entrance showing the contrast in family dynamics

jokes.text: Hey, Homer. Did you just fart? Did you just fart, sir. Whatever.
jokes.setup: Eduardo trying to teach Bart respect
jokes.punchline: Bart's minimal effort and immediate dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's token effort at politeness

jokes.text: I wanted to see who would get here first. Now, Homer! Get your coat.
jokes.setup: Eduardo wanting to leave immediately
jokes.punchline: Homer having ordered competing pizzas to see delivery timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer behavior - turning everything into a food-related experiment

jokes.text: When I return your husband, he will be happy, bringing a new sense of adventure to your marriage... and to the bedroom. I'm not used to strange men saying the word 'bedroom' around me. Would you prefer, uh, 'sala deamor'? That's even worse. Could you say it one more time? Sala de amor. Ooh!
jokes.setup: Eduardo promising to return Homer improved, including romantically
jokes.punchline: Marge's discomfort turning to intrigue with the Spanish version
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how foreign language can make things sound exotic

jokes.text: Would you prefer, uh, 'sala de amor'? That's even worse. Could you say it one more time? Sala de amor. Ooh!
jokes.setup: Eduardo offers Spanish alternative to 'bedroom'
jokes.punchline: Marge finds it more suggestive but still wants to hear it again
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation showing Marge's conflicted attraction to Eduardo's foreign charm

jokes.text: He's not the toughest kid, I am! Now, the smartest kid. Milhouse? He's not the smartest kid. I am. Now the class nerd. Milhouse? Oh.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to get different types of kids to sign the honor code
jokes.punchline: Milhouse only qualifying as the class nerd
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure with perfect character categorization

jokes.text: I took the liberty of crossing off the, uh, stupid ones.
jokes.setup: Eduardo showing Homer his childhood dreams list
jokes.punchline: Most dreams being crossed out as stupid
jokes.explanation: Visual gag implied by dialogue - most of Homer's dreams being deemed stupid

jokes.text: Wait, are you in love with me? In love with the concept of you, yes. Woo-hoo! I'm an attractive concept like Liberty!
jokes.setup: Homer suspecting Eduardo's motives
jokes.punchline: Homer's excitement about being an attractive concept
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor with Homer comparing himself to abstract concepts

jokes.text: I'm sick of pirates off the street ruining my play! I'm going back to my old career! Care to see a dessert menu? We have the best cobbler since Daniel Day-Lewis.
jokes.setup: Shakespeare actor quitting due to interruptions
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'cobbler' meaning both dessert and shoemaker (Day-Lewis role)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting cobbler dessert to Daniel Day-Lewis's shoemaker role

jokes.text: How much did this cost to restage? They rented everything from Comic Book Guy for ten bucks.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about the cost of the pirate show
jokes.punchline: Everything being cheaply rented from Comic Book Guy
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Comic Book Guy character as source of costumes

jokes.text: Don't stink up the Gorn head. I need it for a Bar Mitzvah later.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy protecting his rental items
jokes.punchline: Using a Star Trek alien head for a Bar Mitzvah
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific humor mixing nerd culture with Jewish traditions

jokes.text: Can't you say anything in a normal way? The answer, sadly, is not yes.
jokes.setup: Someone criticizing Skinner's overly formal speech
jokes.punchline: Skinner's response being equally formal and awkward
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character moment - proving the criticism while trying to answer it

jokes.text: I feel kind of, oh... melancholy. Mmm, melon collie.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing feeling melancholy
jokes.punchline: Homer mishearing it as food-related words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - mishearing emotional terms as food words

jokes.text: I am just watching my friend and his wife innocently pleasure themselves in bed. That sounds salty, but you seem sweet. I'm gonna call you Kettle Corn.
jokes.setup: Eduardo explaining why he's watching the Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Ned's sweet/salty personality comparison leading to snack nickname
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with creative wordplay connecting personality to snack foods

jokes.text: You're like the Tooth Fairy. Except you don't collect human bones.
jokes.setup: Homer comparing Eduardo to the Tooth Fairy
jokes.punchline: Dark reframing of tooth collection as bone collecting
jokes.explanation: Dark humor reframing innocent childhood figure in sinister terms

jokes.text: I never leave a job unfinished. It's as true now as that week I worked on the high school yearbook. So much infighting. I had to get out of there.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he never leaves jobs unfinished
jokes.punchline: Immediately describing abandoning a job due to conflict
jokes.explanation: Good ironic structure where Homer immediately contradicts his own statement

jokes.text: The editor put in like six pictures of this girl 'cause she was his girlfriend. Everyone has a bad yearbook story. They spelled my name wrong! Get over it! Ha!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining yearbook drama
jokes.punchline: Eduardo's mild complaint followed by Homer's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about universal yearbook experiences, somewhat rambling

jokes.text: Just step out that door and you can glide to earth like your boyhood hero... Rocky the Flying Squirrel. Uh, actually, my hero was the actress who provided Rocky's voice, June Foray. A true legend in the voice-over community.
jokes.setup: Eduardo assuming Homer's hero was Rocky the Flying Squirrel
jokes.punchline: Homer's unexpectedly specific knowledge about voice acting
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Homer's unexpected depth of knowledge

jokes.text: Will Homer make it? Or will he leave a crater the size of the one that destroyed the Yucatan? Find out in our next exciting installment: Fat Splat... or When You Squish Upon a Car.
jokes.setup: Dramatic narrator cliffhanger about Homer's skydiving
jokes.punchline: Terrible puns for the potential sequel titles
jokes.explanation: Standard parody of adventure serial cliffhangers with weak wordplay

jokes.text: Mmm, it's amazing. Every day has the peace and serenity of a flu outbreak.
jokes.setup: Lisa commenting on the school's new atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Comparing peace to the negative calm of widespread illness
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with a creative comparison between different types of quiet

jokes.text: No, this is good. Well, this knife's got to carve something.
jokes.setup: Lisa declining Willie's offer to carve the pumpkin
jokes.punchline: Willie's ominous implication about needing to use the knife
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor playing on Willie's slightly threatening nature

jokes.text: He got a hundred on his test? That's impossible. Is Bart cheating? Are the Pope's tweets infallible?
jokes.setup: Discovering Bart got a perfect score
jokes.punchline: Lisa's rhetorical question mixing modern technology with religious doctrine
jokes.explanation: Clever modern reference mixing papal infallibility with social media

jokes.text: Because if you tell people I cheated, that means your system failed. Oh, my God, you found a loophole!
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why Lisa can't report his cheating
jokes.punchline: Lisa's horror at Bart finding a logical flaw in her honor system
jokes.explanation: Excellent logic puzzle showing Bart outsmarting Lisa's system

jokes.text: Why don't you put this much inventiveness into your work? Because then I'd be the one thing I swore I wouldn't. You.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why Bart doesn't apply his cleverness to schoolwork
jokes.punchline: Bart's declaration that success would make him like Lisa
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling rivalry moment with Bart's identity-based reasoning

jokes.text: You can listen to your wife when you are dead. Savor the moment.
jokes.setup: Eduardo encouraging Homer to ignore Marge's safety concerns
jokes.punchline: Dark logic about death being the time for spousal obedience
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about marriage dynamics and mortality

jokes.text: Aw, he probably sees a mouse he wants to tear in half.
jokes.setup: Homer admiring a majestic eagle while skydiving
jokes.punchline: Immediately imagining its predatory nature
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's inability to maintain romantic thoughts

jokes.text: You've presented me with quite a conundrum. A word you should know since it was on a vocabulary test you aced.
jokes.setup: Lisa confronting Bart about his cheating
jokes.punchline: Using his fake test success against him
jokes.explanation: Smart writing showing Lisa using Bart's own deception against him

jokes.text: The only thing that'll change my mind is a sign from God.
jokes.setup: Bart claiming nothing will make him confess
jokes.punchline: Setting up for Homer's dramatic crash landing
jokes.explanation: Classic setup line for the dramatic intervention that follows

jokes.text: Son, it was so beautiful! I went faster than the speed of sound.
jokes.setup: Homer crashing through the window to end up next to Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer's impossible claim about exceeding sound speed while falling
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist claim from Homer about his falling speed

jokes.text: This patch is for all the victims of atomic wedgies.
jokes.setup: Bart showing off his detention quilt work
jokes.punchline: Memorializing playground bullying victims
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Bart's artistic dedication to school misbehavior

jokes.text: That's the morphine. Can you give me the morphine forever? No way! A person on morphine all the time would constantly dissolve in inappropriate laughter.
jokes.setup: Homer asking to stay on morphine permanently
jokes.punchline: Doctor's specific description of permanent morphine effects
jokes.explanation: Standard medical humor about drug side effects

jokes.text: Oh. It was all just a dream. It was not just a dream. And you said you'd drive me to the airport.
jokes.setup: Homer thinking Eduardo was a dream
jokes.punchline: Eduardo's real presence and practical request
jokes.explanation: Standard 'it was/wasn't a dream' trope with mundane follow-up

jokes.text: Do I drop you at the curb or do I have to park and walk you in? Go as far as your heart will take you.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about airport drop-off logistics
jokes.punchline: Eduardo's poetic response to a mundane question
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer's practical concern and Eduardo's romantic nature

tmdb_overview: Marge encourages Homer to cross an item off an old to-do list by inviting an old pen pal to visit; Lisa institutes an honor code at school.
